name: Sticks Evans
abstract: Samuel "Sticks" Evans (February 5, 1923 – April 11, 1994) was an American drummer, percussionist, music teacher, arranger and musical director. He was credited variously as Sammy "Stick" Evans, Samie Evans, Sammy Evans, Sammie Evans, Stick Evans, Sticks Evans, and Belton Evans.

concerts.label: 20th Century Innovations: Early Experiments with Jazz

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Well one was Sam... Goulash he died. Sticks Evans. Marty Grupp. Those were the three. A boy by the name of Bill Pede used to teach there. Those were four.

about_quotes.speaker: Jo Jones
about_quotes.evidence: That's why I made Sticks Evans - I said, 'You will not - you are a percussionist.' ... I said, 'You're going to play Radio City.' ... Naturally I was hanging out with Mr. Billy Gladstone and the people at Radio City
